name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of experience in analytical development, method development, and small molecule characterization for drug development. Expertise in chromatographic methods (HPLC, GC-MS, LC-MS), spectroscopic techniques (FT-IR, UV-Vis), and thermal analysis (TGA, DSC) for impurity profiling, stability testing, and quality control. Strong background in method validation, SOP development, and CMC support for pharmaceutical applications. Proven track record managing multiple projects under demanding timelines while maintaining data integrity and regulatory compliance focus.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Develop and optimize analytical methods for small molecule and polymer characterization using HPLC, FT-IR, and thermal analysis to support process development and quality control.
Implement statistical process control (SPC) and design of experiments (DOE) to improve method robustness and establish process control limits for scale-up activities.
Developed and validated analytical methods for small molecule characterization using FT-IR, UV-Vis, HPLC, and mass spectrometry (LC-MS, GC-MS) to assess purity, stability, and degradation pathways.
Performed forced degradation studies and stability testing on organic materials, applying thermal stress (TGA, DSC) and spectroscopic monitoring to characterize degradation kinetics.
Established SOPs for analytical instrumentation and method workflows, ensuring reproducibility and data integrity across multiple research projects.
Managed analytical laboratory operations including HPLC, FT-IR, UV-Vis, and thermal analysis systems; implemented calibration and maintenance protocols ensuring compliance with quality standards.
Trained 15+ researchers on proper analytical techniques, method execution, and data documentation practices aligned with GxP principles.

skills.Analytical Techniques: HPLC, LC-MS, GC-MS, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA, DSC, NMR, GPC
skills.Method Development: Method development, method validation, SOP creation, stability testing, impurity profiling
skills.CMC & Quality: CMC analytical support, quality control testing, specification development, data integrity
skills.Statistical Tools: DOE, SPC, JMP, statistical analysis, Excel (advanced)
skills.Regulatory Knowledge: Familiar with ICH guidelines, cGMP concepts, documentation practices

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

projects.title: Bio-based Polymer Process Development
projects.description: Led chemical process development for novel bio-based polymer systems using statistical experimental design.

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Coatings Program
projects.description: Co-led process development for high-performance molecular thin-film coatings for defense optical applications.
